title: Pacific Biosciences (PACB) Q3 Earnings on the Horizon: Analysts' Insights on Key Performance Measures
text: Analysts on Wall Street projected that Pacific Biosciences of California (PACB) would announce a quarterly loss of -$0.16 per share in Q3 2020, representing an increase of 5.9% year-over-year. The company reached its internal revenue targets with revenue growing sequentially over Q2 2020. In their earnings preview, management indicated they would review Q3 financial and business highlights, provide an update on COVID-19's impact on the business, and share their view of growth opportunities. Management believed they could grow revenue sequentially over Q3 levels, though the amount of growth may have been impacted by the emergence of pandemic-related closures in Europe. Additionally, closures due to the pandemic were expected to potentially impact revenues in the first half of 2021. The earnings call took place on November 2, 2020, with the new CEO Christian Henry sharing early impressions of the company and discussing the business opportunities ahead.
